data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Albanese at SubProjects/Albanese
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- SubProjects/Albanese/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0006/sessions/0007-horizon-T6

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next
- Point the next Albanese-adjacent run at `AJC.pic0av` (`tangentSpaceIso`) or FGA representability, not `ALB.codim1` — see `recommendation.md` / `I-0041`.

# Recommended focus
Task: T6
Title: Albanese self-contained leaves + dead-code cleanup
Project: Albanese
Suggested scope: projects=Albanese; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/StandardSmoothDimension.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean

Attack ONLY the self-contained Albanese sorries — the ones NOT blocked on other cones (A.3/AJC.pic0av, AJC.picrep) or an upstream Mathlib PR. Albanese has its own build dir and is green, so this runs fully parallel to the FBC (T2) AJC run. DO NOT touch AlbaneseUP.lean (7, blocked on A.3), Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean (2, blocked on picrep), or RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean (1, blocked upstream).

Targets:
1. CLEANUP FIRST (safe, verified 2026-07-02): delete the superseded ~250 LOC dead-code chain in CodimOneExtension.lean L647–871 (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_*` → `ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial` → `ringKrullDim_quotient_localization_MvPolynomial_of_regular`) — terminal decl referenced nowhere, fully superseded by StandardSmoothDimension.lean. Re-point any blueprint nodes. Confirm build stays green after deletion.
2. CodimOneExtension.lean (3 sorries): the residual `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` needs Stacks 00OF (localization of a regular local ring is regular — Serre homological characterization); the Milne 3.1/3.3 sorries additionally need 0AVF + function-field pullback machinery. Prove what is reachable; the 00OF route is a large build, so time-box and report if it does not converge.
3. Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean (1 sorry): `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` branch 2 — needs the codim-≥2 conclusion unbundled from Milne 3.1.

Keep the build GREEN throughout; report residuals with precise blockers. See inbox memory I-0027/I-0032 for prior recipes.

# Roadmap slice
- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×1).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- Recipes: closing GeometricallyReduced / GeometricallyIrreducible scaffold sorries (v4.31 Mathlib has the full API)

Mathlib v4.31 ships `AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/{Basic,Reduced,Irreducible}.lean` — these scaffold instances are now provable, not gaps. Proven patterns (see `Points.lean` `gm_geometricallyReduced`, `ChartIso.lean` `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, both axiom-clean):

- **Affine case** (`Spec A ⟶ Spec k̄`): `rw [geometricallyReduced_iff / geometricallyIrreducible_iff]`, intro the `IsPullback`, `obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y; algebraize [φ.hom]`, then `h.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso k̄ A K` reduces to `IsReduced/IsDomain (A ⊗[k̄] K)`. Needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (the `(Gm k̄).hom`-to-`Spec.map` defeq).
- **Ring step for localizations**: `IsReduced (Localization.Away t ⊗[k̄] K)` via `Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange k̄ P P _ K` (`≅ Loc ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K)`), instance `IsLocalization.tensor`, and `isReduced_localizationPreserves` (apply with `by exact` + explicit S — beta-reduction quirk). `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` give `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial σ K`.
- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). Assemble with the new `irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens` (ChartIso).
- **Traps**: inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, ring-level `IsReduced` must be `_root_.IsReduced`; `⊗[k̄]` notation needs `open scoped TensorProduct in` BEFORE the docstring; avoid `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _` (leaves an unsynthesizable eta-expanded `IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms` goal — use `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (iso).hom` or `.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` instead); the affine cover's `I₀`/`𝒰.f i` are semireducibly opaque — work with `Proj.awayι (X i)` + `fin_cases` normalization (`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux` idiom) rather than cover indices.
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker` (note `P.ker` unfolds to `RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)` by rfl-level abbrevs); quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` — a Noetherian local ring with `finrank Cotangent ≤ ringKrullDim` is regular.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis inside later applications (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does — same idiom as Mathlib's `finite_of_algHom_finiteType_of_isJacobsonRing`).

**Remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`: Stacks 00OF** (localisation of regular local is regular) — needs Serre's finite-global-dimension characterisation, absent from Mathlib v4.31 (`IsRegularLocalRing` has only `RegularLocalRing/Defs.lean`). At non-closed points (ii.A) correctly fails (`finrank Cotangent = ht z ≠ n`). Do not attempt 00OF as a side quest — it is a large homological build.
- Recipe: smooth-over-k̄ ⇒ reduced is CLOSED; Stacks 00NP (regular local ⇒ domain) EXISTS in-project — do not re-derive either.

- **`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`** (`AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, public, axiom-clean): any Noetherian regular local ring is a domain. This is the full Stacks `00NP`; docstrings elsewhere calling it a "Mathlib gap" predate its closure. Companions: `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (R/(x) regular of spanFinrank −1 for `x ∈ 𝔪∖𝔪²`), `matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent` (§3.C, cotangent-independent ⇒ regular sequence).
- **Smooth over `Spec k̄` ⇒ reduced**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` (`CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B Step B.f) + ring-level `isReduced_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed` (Step B.e). Proof pattern that works at *every* point (no 00OF needed): reduced is stalk-local (`isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`) + detected at maximal localisations (`isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal`), and B.d′ closed-point regularity + 00NP give those. Contrast: stalk *regularity* at non-closed points stays 00OF-blocked.
- Useful glue found in v4.31 Mathlib: `RingHom.isStandardSmooth_respectsIso` (`.2` = precompose ring equiv — transports a standard-smooth presentation across `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` base identification), `RingHom.isStandardSmooth_algebraMap`, `isReduced_localizationPreserves`, `isReduced_of_noZeroDivisors`.
- Mathlib does NOT have: smooth ⇒ reduced/geometrically-reduced at scheme level (checked `Geometrically/Reduced.lean`, `Morphisms/Smooth.lean`, `RingTheory/Smooth/*`), nor regular-local ⇒ domain (`RegularLocalRing/` has only `Defs.lean`). `Group/Smooth.lean` goes the other way (geom. reduced group scheme ⇒ smooth).
- Iteration trick: a root-level scratch `import Mathlib + import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CodimOneExtension` type-checks in ~4 min against warm oleans; `open private <name> from <module>` lets `#print axioms` reach private decls.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.
- **Open-open pushforward–restriction commutation**: the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` 4-step pattern (`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso(eqToIso) ≪≫ pushforwardCongr ≪≫ pushforwardComp.symm`) transfers verbatim to the square `opensInterLift V₀ U ≫ V₀.ι = (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι`; the appLE-coherence proof is the same 5 lines.

- Pic0Scheme LFT tidy gate NEGATIVE — HasPicScheme packages no finiteness data

1411	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
1412	  have hfin :
1413	      Module.finrank
1414	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
1415	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
1416	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
1417	    rw [hdim] at h
1418	    exact_mod_cast h
1419	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
1420	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
1421	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
1422	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
1423	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
1424	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
1425	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
1426	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
1427	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
1428	    intro hbot
1429	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
1430	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
1431	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
1432	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
1433	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
1434	    rw [hdim] at h0
1435	    exact_mod_cast h0
1436	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
1437	
1438	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
1439	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with

1440	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1441	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
1442	
1443	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
1444	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1445	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1446	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1447	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
1448	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
1449	
1450	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
1451	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
1452	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
1453	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1454	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1455	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1456	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1457	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1458	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1459	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1460	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1461	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1462	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1463	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1464	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1465	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1466	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1467	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1468	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1469	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1470	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
1471	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
1472	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
1473	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
1474	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
1475	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
1476	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1477	    intro hF
1478	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
1479	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
1480	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
1481	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

1485	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1486	
1487	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1488	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1489	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1490	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1491	
1492	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1493	
1494	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1495	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1496	
1497	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1498	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1499	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1500	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1501	
1502	The proof combines two steps:
1503	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1504	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1505	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1506	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1507	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1508	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1509	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1510	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1511	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1512	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1513	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1514	
1515	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1516	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1517	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1518	
1519	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1520	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1521	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1522	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1523	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1524	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1525	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1526	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1527	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1528	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1529	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1530	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1531	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1532	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1533	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1534	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1535	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1536	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1537	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1538	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1539	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1540	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1541	  --
1542	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1543	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1544	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1545	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1546	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1547	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1548	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1549	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1550	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1551	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1552	  --
1553	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1554	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1555	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1556	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1557	  --
1558	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1559	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1560	  sorry

1562	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1563	
1564	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1565	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1566	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1567	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1568	
1569	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1570	
1571	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1572	codim 1.**
1573	
1574	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1575	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1576	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1577	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1578	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1579	
1580	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1581	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1582	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1583	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1584	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1585	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1586	
1587	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1588	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1589	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1590	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1591	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1592	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1593	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1594	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1595	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1596	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1597	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1598	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1599	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1600	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1601	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1602	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1603	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1604	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1605	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1606	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1607	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1608	  --
1609	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1610	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1611	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1612	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1613	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1614	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1615	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1616	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1617	  --   the domain of definition.
1618	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1619	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1620	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1621	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1622	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1623	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1624	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1625	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1626	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1627	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1628	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1629	  --   not packaged.
1630	  --
1631	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1632	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1633	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1634	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).
1635	  sorry

1637	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1638	
1639	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1640	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1641	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1642	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1643	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1644	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1645	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1646	
1647	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1648	
1649	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1650	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1651	
1652	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1653	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1654	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1655	
1656	which requires the pullback machinery
1657	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1658	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1659	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1660	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1661	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1662	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1663	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1664	
1665	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1666	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1667	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1668	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1669	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1670	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1671	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1672	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1673	the pullback machinery lands.
1674	
1675	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1676	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
1677	
1678	end RationalMap
1679	
1680	end Scheme

200	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
201	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
202	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
203	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
204	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
205	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.

208	  -- Step 3: smoothness over the algebraically closed base field forces

214	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
215	Milne's two-input argument.**
216	
217	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
218	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
219	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
220	
221	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
222	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
223	
224	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
225	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
226	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
227	   either empty or pure codim 1.
228	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
229	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
230	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
231	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
232	
233	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
234	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
235	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
236	
237	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
238	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
239	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
240	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
241	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
242	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
243	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
244	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
245	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
246	
247	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
248	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
249	substantive missing content localised here. -/
250	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
251	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
252	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
253	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
254	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
255	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
256	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
257	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
258	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
259	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
260	    CodimOneFree f := by
261	  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
262	  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
263	  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
264	  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
265	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
266	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
267	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
268	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
269	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
270	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
271	  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
272	  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
273	  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
274	  intro x hx
275	  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
276	  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
277	    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
278	    by_contra hnotin
279	    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
280	    rw [hempty] at hxZ
281	    exact hxZ.elim
282	  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
283	    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
284	    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
285	    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
286	    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
287	    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
288	    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
289	    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
290	    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
291	    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
292	    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
293	    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
294	    -- lemma. Once a lemma
295	    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
296	    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:
297	    --   `by_contra hnotin; obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin;`
298	    --   `obtain rfl := sober_eq_of_specializes_of_coheight_eq hxz hx hz1;`
299	    --   `exact absurd hx (lt_irrefl 1 ∘ ... ∘ codimGe2 hnotin)`.
300	    sorry
301	
302	/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
303	The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
304	without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
305	standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
306	the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
307	private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
308	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
309	    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
310	  intro x _
311	  by_contra hnotin
312	  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
313	  rw [h] at hxZ
314	  exact hxZ.elim
315	
316	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
317	for `extend_to_av`.**
318	
319	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
320	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
321	its former `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap now closed axiom-clean) and
322	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
323	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
324	
325	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
326	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
327	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
328	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
329	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
330	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
331	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
332	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
333	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
334	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
335	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
336	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
337	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
338	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
339	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
340	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
341	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
342	
343	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
344	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
345	
346	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
347	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
348	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
349	
350	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
351	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
352	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
353	`J → A`.
354	
355	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
356	Milne's two-line argument:
357	
358	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
359	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
360	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
361	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
362	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
363	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
364	   by the caller.
365	
366	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
367	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
368	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
369	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
370	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
371	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
372	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
373	
374	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
375	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
376	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
377	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
378	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
379	
380	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
381	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
382	theorem extend_to_av
383	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
384	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
385	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
386	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
387	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
388	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
389	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.FaithfullyFlat.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.Module
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free
12	
13	/-!
14	# Formally smooth local algebras
15	-/
16	
17	public section
18	
19	open TensorProduct IsLocalRing KaehlerDifferential
20	
21	variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsLocalRing S] [Algebra R S]
22	
23	namespace Algebra
24	
25	/--
26	The **Jacobian criterion** for smoothness of local algebras.
27	Suppose `S` is a local `R`-algebra, and `0 → I → P → S → 0` is a presentation such that
28	`P` is formally-smooth over `R`, `Ω[P⁄R]` is finite free over `P`,
29	(typically satisfied when `P` is the localization of a polynomial ring of finite type)
30	and `I` is finitely generated.
31	Then `S` is formally smooth iff `k ⊗ₛ I/I² → k ⊗ₚ Ω[P/R]` is injective,
32	where `k` is the residue field of `S`.
33	-/
34	theorem FormallySmooth.iff_injective_lTensor_residueField.{u}
35	    (P : Algebra.Extension.{u} R S)
36	    [FormallySmooth R P.Ring]
37	    [Module.Free P.Ring Ω[P.Ring⁄R]] [Module.Finite P.Ring Ω[P.Ring⁄R]]
38	    (h' : P.ker.FG) :
39	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R S ↔
40	      Function.Injective (P.cotangentComplex.lTensor (ResidueField S)) := by
41	  have : Module.Finite P.Ring P.Cotangent :=
42	    have : Module.Finite P.Ring P.ker := .of_fg h'
43	    .of_surjective _ Extension.Cotangent.mk_surjective
44	  have : Module.Finite S P.Cotangent := Module.Finite.of_restrictScalars_finite P.Ring _ _
45	  rw [← IsLocalRing.split_injective_iff_lTensor_residueField_injective,
46	    P.formallySmooth_iff_split_injection]
47	
48	theorem FormallySmooth.iff_injective_cotangentComplexBaseChange_residueField
49	    (P : Type*) [CommRing P] [Algebra R P] [Algebra P S]
50	    [IsScalarTower R P S] [FormallySmooth R P] [Module.Free P Ω[P⁄R]] [Module.Finite P Ω[P⁄R]]
51	    (h₁ : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P S)) (h₂ : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P S)).FG) :
52	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R S ↔
53	      Function.Injective (cotangentComplexBaseChange R S P (ResidueField S)) := by
54	  let P' : Extension R S := { Ring := P, σ := _, algebraMap_σ := Function.surjInv_eq h₁ }
55	  rw [Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_injective_lTensor_residueField P' h₂]
56	  rw [P'.cotangentComplexBaseChange_eq_lTensor_cotangentComplex (ResidueField S)]
57	  refine .trans ?_ ((AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange P'.Ring S _ _
58	    Ω[P'.Ring⁄R]).comp_injective _).symm
59	  exact (((AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange P'.Ring S _ _ P'.ker).symm ≪≫ₗ
60	    P'.cotangentEquiv.baseChange (A := _)).injective_comp _).symm
61	
62	/--
63	The **Jacobian criterion** for smoothness of local algebras.
64	Suppose `S` is a local `R`-algebra, and `0 → I → P → S → 0` is a presentation such that
65	`P` is formally-smooth over `R`, `Ω[P⁄R]` is finite free over `P`,
66	(typically satisfied when `P` is the localization of a polynomial ring of finite type)
67	and `I` is finitely generated.
68	Then `S` is formally smooth iff `k ⊗ₛ I → k ⊗ₚ Ω[P/R]` is injective,
69	where `k` any field extension of the residue field of `S`.
70	-/
71	theorem FormallySmooth.iff_injective_cotangentComplexBaseChange
72	    (P K : Type*) [Field K] [CommRing P] [Algebra R P] [Algebra P S]
73	    [IsScalarTower R P S] [Algebra S K] [Algebra P K] [IsScalarTower P S K]
74	    [FormallySmooth R P] [Module.Free P Ω[P⁄R]] [Module.Finite P Ω[P⁄R]]
75	    (h₁ : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P S)) (h₂ : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P S)).FG)
76	    (h₃ : maximalIdeal S ≤ RingHom.ker (algebraMap S K)) :
77	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R S ↔ Function.Injective (cotangentComplexBaseChange R S P K) := by
78	  let f : ResidueField S →ₐ[S] K := Ideal.Quotient.liftₐ _ (Algebra.ofId _ _) h₃
79	  let := f.toAlgebra
80	  have := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' f.comp_algebraMap.symm
81	  have : IsScalarTower P (ResidueField S) K := .to₁₃₄ _ S _ _
82	  rw [FormallySmooth.iff_injective_cotangentComplexBaseChange_residueField P h₁ h₂,
83	    ← Module.FaithfullyFlat.lTensor_injective_iff_injective _ K]
84	  have : (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
85	      (cotangentComplexBaseChange R S P (ResidueField S)).baseChange K ∘ₗ
86	      (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _).symm.toLinearMap =
87	      (cotangentComplexBaseChange R S P K) := by
88	    ext
89	    #adaptation_note /-- Prior to nightly-2026-04-06, this was just `simp`. -/
90	    simp_rw [AlgebraTensorModule.curry_apply, LinearMap.restrictScalars_comp, curry_apply,
91	      LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply,
92	      AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_symm_tmul, LinearMap.baseChange_tmul,
93	      cotangentComplexBaseChange_tmul, kerToTensor_apply, one_smul,
94	      AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_tmul]
95	    simp
96	  rw [← this]
97	  refine .trans ?_ ((AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _).comp_injective _).symm
98	  exact ((AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ _ _ _).symm.injective_comp _).symm
99	
100	end Algebra

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2025 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Field
public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.SeparablyGenerated

/-!

# Smooth algebras over fields

We show that separably generated extensions of fields are smooth.
In particular finitely generated field extensions over perfect fields are smooth.

-/

public section

variable {K L ι : Type*} [Field L] [Field K] [Algebra K L]

open scoped IntermediateField.algebraAdjoinAdjoin in
lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) :
    Algebra.FormallySmooth K (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) := by
  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth K (adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
    .of_equiv hb.aevalEquiv
  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth (adjoin K (Set.range v))
      (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
    .of_isLocalization (nonZeroDivisors _)
  exact .comp _ (adjoin K (Set.range v)) _

/-- Purely transcendental extensions are formally smooth. -/
lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) (hb' : IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v) = ⊤) :
    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
  have := Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
  rw [hb'] at this
  exact .of_equiv IntermediateField.topEquiv

/-- Separably generated extensions are formally smooth. -/
lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent_of_isSeparable
    {v : ι → L} (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v)
    [Algebra.IsSeparable (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L] :
    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
  have := FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
  have : FormallyEtale (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L :=
    Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable _ L
  exact .comp _ (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) _

instance (priority := low) Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField
    [PerfectField K] [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L] : Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
  obtain ⟨s, hs, H⟩ := exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField K L
  have : Algebra.IsSeparable (↥(IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range ((↑) : s → L)))) L := by
    convert! H <;> simp
  exact .of_algebraicIndependent_of_isSeparable hs.1
===

3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Krull dimension at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras (Stacks 00OE substrate)
10	
11	This file discharges **sub-gap (ii.B)** of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline of
12	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (Stacks tag `00OE`, the smooth-algebra
13	Krull-dimension formula), in the closed-point form the pipeline consumes: for a
14	standard-smooth algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a
15	*maximal* ideal `m ⊆ S`, the localisation `Sₘ` has Krull dimension (at least,
16	hence — combined with the cotangent bound — exactly) `n`.
17	
18	Contrary to the iter-199 estimate ("~200–300 LOC on `transcendenceDegree` +
19	Noether normalisation"), no transcendence-degree theory is needed: the v4.31
20	Mathlib toolbox (`Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`, Krull's height theorem
21	in the form `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`, and the
22	`MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance) supports a direct height computation:
23	
24	* `MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`: every maximal ideal of
25	  `MvPolynomial ι k` (finitely many variables over a field) has height
26	  `Nat.card ι`. Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`; the
27	  induction step contracts a maximal ideal of `R[X]` to a maximal ideal of `R`
28	  (Jacobson) and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`.
29	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal`:
30	  the **lower bound** `n ≤ ht m` for a maximal ideal `m` of a standard-smooth
31	  `k`-algebra of relative dimension `n`. Writing `S = P.Ring ⧸ ker` for a
32	  submersive presentation `P` with `#ι` generators and `#σ` relations
33	  (`n = #ι - #σ`), the pullback `M ⊆ P.Ring` of `m` is maximal of height `#ι`,
34	  and Krull's height theorem bounds `ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`.
35	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`:
36	  the same lower bound for `ringKrullDim Sₘ` at the localisation.
37	* `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`: the generic
38	  regularity glue — a Noetherian local ring whose cotangent-space dimension is
39	  bounded by its Krull dimension is regular (the reverse inequality is
40	  automatic).
41	
42	The consumer (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` Stage 6 in
43	`CodimOneExtension.lean`) combines the last two lemmas with the iter-199
44	sub-gap (ii.A) helper `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
45	(`finrank κ(m) (m/m²) = n` at a `k`-rational closed point) to conclude
46	regularity of the stalk at closed points. The remaining gap for *non-closed*
47	points is Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular),
48	tracked in `CodimOneExtension.lean`.
49	
50	Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`
51	(nodes `lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`,
52	`lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal`,
53	`lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization`,
54	`lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u v
58	
59	open Ideal
60	
61	section MvPolynomialHeight
62	
63	/-- **Maximal ideals of finite-variable polynomial rings over a field have full
64	height.** For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι` and a maximal ideal
65	`M ⊆ k[Xᵢ : i ∈ ι]`, the height of `M` is `Nat.card ι`.
66	
67	Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`: the base case is the
68	field `k` itself (Krull dimension `0`), and the induction step writes
69	`MvPolynomial (Option α) k ≃+* (MvPolynomial α k)[X]`, contracts the maximal
70	ideal along `C` (maximal again, since `MvPolynomial α k` is Jacobson), and
71	applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`. -/
72	theorem MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal
73	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ι : Type v} [Finite ι]
74	    (M : Ideal (MvPolynomial ι k)) (hM : M.IsMaximal) :
75	    M.height = Nat.card ι := by
76	  induction ι using Finite.induction_empty_option with
77	  | of_equiv e IH =>
78	    haveI := hM
79	    have h1 := IH (M.comap (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k e).toRingEquiv)
80	      (Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_equiv _)
81	    rw [RingEquiv.height_comap] at h1
82	    rw [h1, Nat.card_congr e]
83	  | h_empty =>
84	    have hdim : ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial PEmpty.{v + 1} k) = 0 := by
85	      rw [ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyRingEquiv k PEmpty)]
86	      exact ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field k
87	    have hle : (M.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ 0 :=
88	      hdim ▸ Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hM.ne_top
89	    have hz : M.height = 0 := le_antisymm (by exact_mod_cast hle) (by simp)
90	    simp [hz]
91	  | h_option IH =>
92	    rename_i α _
93	    haveI := hM
94	    set ψ := (MvPolynomial.optionEquivLeft k α).toRingEquiv with hψ
95	    set P' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial α k)) := M.map ψ with hP'
96	    haveI hP'max : P'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv ψ
97	    set p : Ideal (MvPolynomial α k) := P'.comap (Polynomial.C) with hpdef
98	    haveI hpmax : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing P'
99	    haveI : P'.LiesOver p := ⟨by rw [hpdef, Ideal.under_def, Polynomial.algebraMap_eq]⟩
100	    have hht : P'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p P'
101	    have hIH : p.height = Nat.card α := IH p hpmax
102	    have hMP' : M.height = P'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map ψ M).symm
103	    have hcard : Nat.card (Option α) = Nat.card α + 1 := by
104	      simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
105	    rw [hMP', hht, hIH, hcard]
106	    push_cast
107	    ring
108	
109	end MvPolynomialHeight
110	
111	section StandardSmoothLowerBound
112	
113	namespace Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
114	
115	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound (closed-point form).** For a standard-smooth
116	algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a maximal ideal
117	`m ⊆ S`, the height of `m` is at least `n`.
118	
119	Proof: extract a submersive presentation `P` (with `#ι` generators, `#σ`
120	relations, `n = #ι - #σ`). The pullback `M` of `m` to the polynomial ring
121	`P.Ring` is maximal of height `#ι`
122	(`MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`), contains the kernel of the
123	presentation (generated by the `#σ` relations), and Krull's height theorem
124	(`Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`) gives
125	`#ι = ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`, whence `n ≤ ht m`. -/
126	theorem natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal
127	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
128	    [H : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
129	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
130	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, hσ, hι, P, hPdim⟩ := H.out
131	  haveI := hm
132	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P.Ring S) := P.algebraMap_surjective
133	  set M : Ideal P.Ring := m.comap (algebraMap P.Ring S) with hMdef
134	  haveI hM : M.IsMaximal := Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ hsurj
135	  -- full height of the pullback in the polynomial ring
136	  have hMheight : M.height = Nat.card ι := MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal M hM
137	  -- the presentation kernel sits inside the pullback
138	  have hker_le : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) ≤ M := fun x hx => by
139	    rw [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap, RingHom.mem_ker.mp hx]
140	    exact m.zero_mem
141	  -- Krull's height theorem
142	  have hbound := Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le hker_le
143	  -- identify the quotient-side prime with `m` (first isomorphism theorem)
144	  set e := (algebraMap P.Ring S : P.Ring →+* S).quotientKerEquivOfSurjective hsurj with hedef
145	  have hMcomap : M = Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))
146	      (Ideal.comap e m) := by
147	    ext x
148	    simp only [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap]
149	    rw [hedef, RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk]
150	  have hqheight : (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
151	      = m.height := by
152	    rw [hMcomap, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective,
153	      RingEquiv.height_comap]
154	  -- the kernel is generated by the `#σ` relations
155	  have hker_span : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) = Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) :=
156	    P.span_range_relation_eq_ker.symm
157	  have hfr : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank ≤ Nat.card σ := by
158	    rw [hker_span]
159	    refine le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_range _)) ?_
160	    calc (Set.range P.relation).ncard
161	        = (P.relation '' Set.univ).ncard := by rw [Set.image_univ]
162	      _ ≤ (Set.univ : Set σ).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le Set.finite_univ
163	      _ = Nat.card σ := Set.ncard_univ σ
164	  -- cardinal bookkeeping: `n + #σ = #ι`
165	  have hcards : n + Nat.card σ = Nat.card ι := by
166	    have hle : Nat.card σ ≤ Nat.card ι := Nat.card_le_card_of_injective P.map P.map_inj
167	    have hdim : Nat.card ι - Nat.card σ = n := hPdim
168	    omega
169	  -- combine
170	  have h1 : (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
171	    calc (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) = M.height := hMheight.symm
172	      _ ≤ (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
173	          + (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank := hbound
174	      _ ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
175	          rw [hqheight]
176	          have hfr' : ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank : ℕ∞)
177	              ≤ (Nat.card σ : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hfr
178	          exact add_le_add le_rfl hfr'
179	  rw [← hcards] at h1
180	  push_cast at h1
181	  exact (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by simp)).mp h1
182	
183	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound, localised form.** For a standard-smooth algebra
184	`S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k`, a maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` and a
185	localisation `Sₘ` of `S` at `m`, the Krull dimension of `Sₘ` is at least `n`. -/
186	theorem le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
187	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
188	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
189	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
190	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m] :
191	    (n : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ := by
192	  haveI := hm.isPrime
193	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m Sₘ]
194	  exact_mod_cast natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal (k := k) n m hm
195	
196	end Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
197	
198	end StandardSmoothLowerBound
199	
200	section RegularityGlue
201	
202	/-- **Regularity from the cotangent upper bound.** A Noetherian local ring whose
203	cotangent-space dimension is bounded above by its Krull dimension is a regular
204	local ring (the reverse inequality `dim ≤ dim_κ m/m²` holds in any Noetherian
205	local ring, via `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`). This is the glue
206	between the sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation and the sub-gap (ii.B)
207	dimension lower bound in the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline. -/
208	theorem IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
209	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
210	    (h : (Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace A) : WithBot ℕ∞)
211	      ≤ ringKrullDim A) : IsRegularLocalRing A := by
212	  apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
213	  rwa [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
214	
215	end RegularityGlue

401	lemma ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard (s : Set R) (hs : s ⊆ Ring.jacobson R) :
402	    ringKrullDim R ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span s) + s.encard := by
403	  refine le_trans (ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_spanFinrank (Ideal.span s) ?_) ?_
404	  · simpa [Ideal.span_le]
405	  · gcongr; norm_cast; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_encard _
406	
407	lemma ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_card (s : Finset R)
408	    (hs : (s : Set R) ⊆ Ring.jacobson R) :
409	    ringKrullDim R ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.span (s : Set R)) + s.card := by
410	  convert! ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard s hs
411	  norm_cast
412	
413	section Algebra
414	
415	variable {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
416	
417	/--
418	If `P` lies over `p`, the height of `P` is bounded by the height of `p` plus
419	the height of the image of `P` in `S ⧸ p S`.
420	Equality holds if `S` satisfies going-down as an `R`-algebra
421	(see `Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown`).
422	-/
423	@[stacks 00OM]
424	lemma Ideal.height_le_height_add_of_liesOver [IsNoetherianRing S] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
425	      (P : Ideal S) [P.IsPrime] [P.LiesOver p] :
426	    P.height ≤ p.height +
427	      (P.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk <| p.map (algebraMap R S))).height := by
428	  classical
429	  obtain ⟨s, hp, heq⟩ := p.exists_finset_card_eq_height_of_isNoetherianRing
430	  let P' := P.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk <| p.map (algebraMap R S))
431	  obtain ⟨s', hP', heq'⟩ := P'.exists_finset_card_eq_height_of_isNoetherianRing
432	  have hsP'sub : (s' : Set <| S ⧸ (Ideal.map (algebraMap R S) p)) ⊆ (P' : Set <| S ⧸ _) :=
433	    fun x hx ↦ hP'.le (Ideal.subset_span hx)
434	  have : Set.SurjOn (Ideal.Quotient.mk (p.map (algebraMap R S))) P s' := by
435	    refine Set.SurjOn.mono subset_rfl hsP'sub fun x hx ↦ ?_
436	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
437	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, Ideal.mem_quotient_iff_mem] at hx
438	    · use y, hx
439	    · rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap, Ideal.LiesOver.over (p := p) (P := P)]
440	  obtain ⟨o, ho, hinj, himgo⟩ := s'.exists_subset_injOn_image_eq_of_surjOn (P : Set S) this
441	  let t : Finset S := Finset.image (algebraMap R S) s ∪ o
442	  suffices h : P.height ≤ t.card by
443	    rw [← heq, ← heq']
444	    apply le_trans h
445	    norm_cast
446	    refine le_trans (Finset.card_union_le _ _) (add_le_add Finset.card_image_le ?_)
447	    rw [← himgo, Finset.card_image_of_injOn hinj]
448	  refine Ideal.height_le_card_of_mem_minimalPrimes_span_finset ?_
449	  have : Ideal.span t = Ideal.map (algebraMap R S) (.span s) ⊔ .span o := by
450	    simp [t, Ideal.map_span]
451	  refine this ▸ map_sup_mem_minimalPrimes_of_map_quotientMk_mem_minimalPrimes hp (span_le.mpr ho) ?_
452	  convert! hP'
453	  simp [Ideal.map_span, ← himgo]
454	
455	/--
456	If `S` satisfies going-down as an `R`-algebra and `P` lies over `p`, the height of `P` is equal
457	to the height of `p` plus the height of the image of `P` in `S ⧸ p S`
458	(Matsumura 13.B Th. 19 (2)).
459	-/
460	@[stacks 00ON]
461	lemma Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown [IsNoetherianRing S]
462	    [Algebra.HasGoingDown R S] (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] (P : Ideal S) [P.IsPrime] [P.LiesOver p] :
463	    P.height = p.height +
464	      (P.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk <| p.map (algebraMap R S))).height := by
465	  refine le_antisymm (height_le_height_add_of_liesOver p P) ?_
466	  obtain ⟨lp, hlp, hlenp⟩ := p.exists_ltSeries_length_eq_height
467	  obtain ⟨lq, hlq, hlenq⟩ :=
468	    (P.map (Quotient.mk (p.map (algebraMap R S)))).exists_ltSeries_length_eq_height
469	  let l' : LTSeries (PrimeSpectrum S) :=
470	    lq.map (PrimeSpectrum.comap (Quotient.mk (p.map (algebraMap R S))))
471	      (RingHom.strictMono_comap_of_surjective Quotient.mk_surjective)
472	  have : l'.head.asIdeal.LiesOver lp.last.asIdeal := by
473	    simp only [LTSeries.head_map, hlp, l']
474	    refine ⟨?_⟩
475	    refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
476	    · rw [← map_le_iff_le_comap, PrimeSpectrum.comap_asIdeal, ← map_le_iff_le_comap]
477	      simp
478	    · conv_rhs => rw [LiesOver.over (p := p) (P := P), under_def]
479	      refine comap_mono (le_trans (comap_mono (lq.head_le_last)) ?_)
480	      simp [hlq, map_le_iff_le_comap, LiesOver.over (p := p) (P := P)]
481	  obtain ⟨lp', hlp'len, hlp', _⟩ := exists_ltSeries_of_hasGoingDown lp l'.head.asIdeal
482	  have : (lp'.smash l' hlp').length = lp.length + lq.length := by simp [hlp'len, l']
483	  rw [← hlenp, ← hlenq, ← Nat.cast_add, ← this, (⟨P, ‹_›⟩ : PrimeSpectrum S).height_eq_orderHeight]
484	  apply Order.length_le_height
485	  simp [hlq, l', ← PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal_le_asIdeal, map_le_iff_le_comap,
486	    LiesOver.over (p := p) (P := P)]
487	
488	variable (R) in
489	lemma ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal [IsLocalRing R] :
490	    ringKrullDim R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank :=
491	  le_of_eq_of_le IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_height_eq_ringKrullDim.symm
492	    (WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr (Ideal.height_le_spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
493	      Ideal.IsPrime.ne_top'))
494	
495	end Algebra
496

280	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
281	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
