287	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
288	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
289	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
290	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
291	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
292	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
293	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
294	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
295	
296	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
297	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
298	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
299	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
300	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
301	
302	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
303	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
304	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
305	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
306	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
307	maneuver. -/
308	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
309	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
310	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
311	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
312	    (M : Submonoid S)
313	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
314	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
315	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
316	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
317	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
318	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
319	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
320	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
321	
322	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
323	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
324	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
325	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
326	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
327	
328	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
329	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
330	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
331	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
332	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
333	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
334	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
335	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
336	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
337	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
338	    (M : Submonoid S)
339	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
340	    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
341	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
342	    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
343	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
344	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
345	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
346	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
347	  have hrank :
348	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
349	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
350	    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M
351	      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
352	  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
353	        (S := S) n] at hrank
354	  simpa using hrank
355	
356	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, RHS of the conormal iso).** Iter-198
357	Lane COE axiom-clean substrate helper: under the Stage 5a + 5b hypotheses
358	(`Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` is `Sₘ`-free with `Sₘ`-rank `n`), the residue-field base-change
359	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` has `κ(mₘ)`-dimension `n`. This is the *right-hand
360	side* of the Stacks-02JK cotangent iso
361	`κ(mₘ) ⊗_{Sₘ} Ω[Sₘ⁄R] ≃ mₘ/mₘ²` (the iso itself is the missing Mathlib
362	bridge for sub-lemma 6.B); combined with the bridge it gives
363	`Module.finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n`, the cotangent input of the
364	regularity criterion `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
365	
366	Axiom-clean: composes `Module.finrank_baseChange` (Mathlib re-export,
367	`LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Constructions.lean`) with the cardinal-to-natural
368	cast `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`. Consumes no Mathlib gap.
369	
370	The hypothesis pattern (free + rank = n) is exactly the conclusion of
371	Stages 5a + 5b applied to the stalk of a smooth scheme; the helper is
372	phrased independently of `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` because the
373	stalk-side localisation is not preserved by that class (Mathlib commit
374	`b80f227` only provides `localization_away` which collapses to rel-dim 0).
375	-/
376	private theorem finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq
377	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
378	    {Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
379	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])] (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
380	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
381	      (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
382	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]
383	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
384	
385	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, LHS-RHS bridge): formally-smooth residue
386	gives the cotangent iso.** Iter-199 Lane COE axiom-clean closed-point-style
387	helper: assuming the source ring `Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, its residue
388	field `κ = ResidueField Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, and `Ω[κ⁄R]` is
389	subsingleton (the three hypotheses combine to model the closed-point case of
390	Stacks 02JK over an algebraically closed base, where `κ = R` makes both FS
391	and `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` automatic), the canonical Mathlib map
392	`KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ`
393	is an `Sₘ`-linear equivalence between
394	`(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the `Sₘ`-side packaging of `m/m²`)
395	and `κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` (the residue-field base-change of the Kähler
396	differentials).
397	
398	This is the closed-point case of the Stacks-02JK cotangent ↔ Kähler iso
399	(sub-gap (ii.A) of the Stage 6 chain in `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`).
400	The proof is the 3-step recipe from `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md`:
401	
402	* **Step 1** — retraction → injection via
403	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: under
404	  `FormallySmooth R κ` and surjectivity of `Sₘ → κ`, the conormal map
405	  `kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ` admits a left-inverse `l`, hence is injective.
406	* **Step 2** — `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection: combining
407	  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` with
408	  `Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R])` gives that every element of the tensor space lies
409	  in the kernel of `mapBaseChange`, hence in the range of `kerCotangentToTensor`.
410	* **Step 3** — bijective → iso: package as `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`.
411	
412	The `kerCotangentToTensor` map's domain is
413	`(RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent`, which equals
414	`(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent = IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` since
415	`κ = Sₘ / maximalIdeal Sₘ`; the equivalence is therefore the
416	Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent iso
417	`m/m² ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`.
418	
419	Axiom-clean: composes
420	`Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` (Mathlib;
421	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Basic.lean`),
422	`KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` (Mathlib;
423	`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`), and `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`. -/
424	private noncomputable def
425	    cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
426	    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
427	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
428	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
429	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
430	    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
431	      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
432	  -- Surjectivity of `algebraMap Sₘ κ` is the residue surjection.
433	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
434	      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
435	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
436	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
437	  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via `iff_split_injection`.
438	  have hInj : Function.Injective
439	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
440	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
441	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
442	        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
443	    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
444	    have h := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
445	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply] at h
446	    exact h
447	  -- Step 2: `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection.
448	  have hExact :=
449	    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
450	      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
451	  have hSurjMap :
452	      Function.Surjective
453	        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
454	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
455	    intro x
456	    have h0 :
457	        KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ
458	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
459	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
460	    exact (hExact x).mp h0
461	  -- Step 3: bijective → linear equivalence.
462	  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩
463	
464	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), maximal-ideal-domain repackaging.** The
465	same Stacks-02JK closed-point cotangent iso as
466	`cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue` above, but
467	restated with `(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the canonical
468	`Sₘ/m = κ`-module side of `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ`) as the domain
469	rather than `(RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent`. The two coincide
470	through `IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq + IsLocalRing.ker_residue`,
471	but the maximal-ideal form is the one Mathlib pre-equips with the κ-module
472	instance via `IsLocalRing.instModuleResidueFieldCotangentSpace`, so this
473	repackaging is what downstream κ-finrank computations want to consume.
474	
475	Axiom-clean: `rw`-transports the iso above along the ideal equality. -/
476	private noncomputable def
477	    cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
478	    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
479	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
480	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
481	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
482	    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
483	      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
484	  have hker : RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) =
485	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ := by
486	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.ker_residue]
487	  rw [← hker]
488	  exact cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
489	
490	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), κ-finrank conclusion.** Assemble the
491	maximal-ideal-side cotangent iso of
492	`cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`
493	with the 6.B-RHS substrate
494	`finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq` to compute
495	the κ-finrank of `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` (= `m/m²`) explicitly.
496	
497	Under the closed-point-style hypothesis pattern (`Sₘ` formally smooth over
498	`R`, residue field `κ` formally smooth over `R`, `Ω[κ⁄R]` subsingleton),
499	combined with `Module.Free Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` of `Sₘ`-rank `n` (the conclusion of
500	Stages 5a + 5b applied to a standard-smooth localisation), the cotangent
501	space `m/m²` has `κ`-finrank equal to `n`.
502	
503	This is the precise conclusion the consumer
504	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` needs: combined with the still-open
505	Stacks-00OE Krull-dim formula (sub-gap (ii.B)) it discharges the regularity
506	criterion `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`. Sub-gap (ii.A)
507	(this lemma) is now axiom-clean; sub-gap (ii.B) remains the residual gap.
508	
509	Axiom-clean: composes the Sₘ-linear iso with `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective`
510	(promotion to κ-linear via the residue surjection), `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`,
511	and the 6.B-RHS substrate `finrank_baseChange + finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`. -/
512	private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
513	    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
514	    [Algebra R Sₘ]
515	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
516	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
517	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])]
518	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
519	    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
520	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
521	      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
522	  -- 6.B-RHS substrate: finrank κ (κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n.
523	  have hRHS :
524	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
525	        (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n :=
526	    finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq n hrank
527	  -- Sₘ-linear iso (maximalIdeal form).
528	  have hiso :
529	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
530	        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
531	    cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
532	  -- Promote to κ-linear via the residue surjection.
533	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
534	      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
535	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
536	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
537	  have hisoκ :
538	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ]
539	        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
540	    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective hSurj hiso
541	  rw [hisoκ.finrank_eq, hRHS]
542	
543	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), bundled bijective-residue-map form.**
544	Closed-point-friendly bundled variant of
545	`finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` that replaces the three
546	typeclass hypotheses `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)` and
547	`Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])` with the single hypothesis that
548	`algebraMap R (ResidueField Sₘ)` is bijective. This is the precise hypothesis
549	the closed-point case of Stacks 02JK consumes: at a `k̄`-rational closed
550	point of a smooth `k̄`-algebra, the residue map `k̄ → κ` is the identity
551	(Nullstellensatz), hence bijective.
552	
553	The proof:
554	
555	* `RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective` + `RingHom.formallySmooth_algebraMap`
556	  upgrade `Bijective` to `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)`.
557	* `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` deduces
558	  `Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])` from the surjectivity half of
559	  bijectivity.
560	* Then apply the typeclass-hypothesis form
561	  `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue`.
562	
563	Axiom-clean: composes Mathlib bridges with the iter-199 helper above. -/
564	private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue
565	    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
566	    [Algebra R Sₘ]
567	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
568	    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
569	    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)))
570	    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
571	    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
572	      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
573	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) :=
574	    RingHom.formallySmooth_algebraMap.mp
575	      (RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective hbij)
576	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R]) :=
577	    KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective R _ hbij.surjective
578	  exact finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue n hrank
579	
580	/-! ### Stacks-00OE Krull-dimension bridge
581	
582	The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at closed points (Stacks 00OE) is
583	provided by `AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension`
584	(`MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal` +
585	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal` +
586	`le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`), which replaced an earlier
587	in-file regular-sequence-witness chain (deleted as dead code). Only the
588	localization/height translator below remains here. -/
589

589	
590	/-- **Stacks 00OE localization/height bridge.** Ergonomic re-export of
591	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
592	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
593	translator between `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183
594	CoheightBridge bridge, phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the
595	`StandardSmoothDimension` height arithmetic (phrased in `Ideal.height`).
596	
597	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
598	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
599	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
600	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
601	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
602	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A
603	
604	/-- **Stage 6 sub-gap (i) resolution (iter-198).** Axiom-clean substrate helper:
605	every `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance can be promoted to
606	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` for some specific `n : ℕ`
607	(extracted from the underlying submersive presentation via
608	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`).
609	
610	This closes the iter-193 "sub-gap (i): relative-dimension determination"
611	identified in the docstring of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` below.
612	The remaining Stage 6 gap is solely (ii) the Stacks-00OE smooth-algebra
613	Krull-dimension formula plus the Stacks-02JK cotangent-Kähler bridge over a
614	field.
615	
616	Axiom-clean: a 4-line unpacking of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.out` via
617	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`. No
618	Mathlib gap; the only reason this was a sub-gap iter-191--194 was an
619	overconservative read of `IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension`'s
620	`Classical.choice` apparatus. -/
621	private theorem exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth
622	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
623	    [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
624	    ∃ n : ℕ, Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
625	  obtain ⟨iota, sigma, hσ, hι, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hS.out
626	  exact ⟨P.dimension, P.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension rfl⟩
627	
628	/-! ## §3.B. Project-local Mathlib supplement — Stage 6 scheme-to-algebra bridges (iter-202)
629	
630	Lane COE Step B substrate: four axiom-clean scheme-to-algebra bridges feeding the
631	eventual closure of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (the L1061 Stacks-00TT
632	regularity gap). They are deliberately decoupled from Step A1 (the Matsumura
633	Jacobian-regular-sequence witness, gated on the iter-203 `AuslanderBuchsbaum`
634	public promotions) so they land independently this iter.
635	
636	* **B.a** (`exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`):
637	  extract the explicit `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` with `P.dimension = n`
638	  from `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` (the relative-dimension
639	  packaging produced by the iter-198 sub-gap (i) discharger
640	  `exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth`).
641	* **B.b** (`isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen`): the affine-open
642	  stalk-localisation `IsLocalization.AtPrime (stalk z) (primeIdealOf z)`, decoupled
643	  from standard-smoothness, as a named reusable substrate lemma.
644	* **B.c** (`open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv`): on a scheme
645	  whose carrier is a subsingleton (e.g.\ `Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`), every
646	  nonempty open is `⊤`; hence the sections of `Spec (.of k̄)` over any nonempty
647	  open form a ring isomorphic to `k̄`. This is the `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄`
648	  definitional bridge of the COE recipe, giving the algebraically-closed base ring
649	  of the standard-smooth presentation a clean `k̄`-identification.
650	
651	`isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen` / `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` are
652	exposed (non-`private`) since they are generic Mathlib-shaped facts a future
653	in-file or cross-file consumer may want; the rest are `private` helpers. -/
654	
655	/-- **Lane COE Step B.a (iter-202).** Extract the explicit
656	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` underlying an
657	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` instance, together with the
658	witness `P.dimension = n`. This is a forward-ergonomics re-export of the
659	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.out` field, giving Step B.d direct
660	access to the submersive presentation `P` (whose relations are the input to the
661	iter-203 Step A1 Jacobian-regular-sequence witness). Axiom-clean: 1-line
662	re-export of the structure field. -/
663	private theorem exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
664	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
665	    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
666	    ∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
667	      ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n :=
668	  h.out
669	
670	/-- **Lane COE Step B.b (iter-202).** The affine-open stalk localisation: for an
671	affine open `V ∋ z` of a scheme `X`, the stalk `X.presheaf.stalk z` is the
672	localisation of the section ring `Γ(X, V)` at the prime ideal `primeIdealOf z`.
673	Re-export of `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`, decoupled here from
674	standard-smoothness so downstream regularity computations (Step B.d) can name the
675	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` instance without re-deriving the Stage-3 algebra package.
676	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
677	theorem isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen
678	    {X : Scheme.{u}} {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (z : X) (hzV : z ∈ V) :
679	    letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
680	    IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal :=
681	  hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
682	
683	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c helper (iter-202).** On a scheme whose underlying carrier
684	is a subsingleton (the topological space has at most one point — e.g.\
685	`Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`, which has a unique point by
686	`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), every nonempty open subset equals `⊤`. Axiom-clean:
687	the unique-point argument via `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
688	private lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme.{u}} [Subsingleton X]
689	    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
690	  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
691	  ext y
692	  simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
693	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]
694	
695	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c (iter-202).** The `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄` bridge: for a
696	field `k̄` and any nonempty open `U` of `Spec (.of k̄)`, the section ring over `U`
697	is ring-isomorphic to `k̄`. Since `Spec (.of k̄)` has a subsingleton carrier
698	(`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), `U = ⊤` and the sections collapse to the global
699	sections `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), ⊤) ≅ k̄` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`). This supplies the
700	algebraically-closed base ring of the standard-smooth presentation
701	(`R = Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U)`) with a clean `k̄`-identification — the geometric input
702	to the closed-point residue-field bridge `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`.
703	Axiom-clean: `subst` of `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
704	noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
705	    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
706	    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
707	  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
708	  subst h
709	  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
710	
711	/-- **Lane COE Step B.d — Stacks `00TT` at a rational closed point, algebra
712	level (this iter).** For a standard-smooth algebra `S` over a field `k`, a
713	maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` whose induced residue map `k → κ(m)` is bijective (the
714	`k`-rational-point condition, automatic over an algebraically closed field by
715	the Nullstellensatz), the localisation `Sₘ` is a regular local ring.
716	
717	This *closes sub-gap (ii.B) at closed points*: the proof combines
718	* the iter-199 sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation
719	  `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
720	  (`finrank κ (m/m²) = n`, from formal smoothness + free Kähler differentials
721	  of rank `n`), with
722	* the new `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` dimension lower bound
723	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`
724	  (`n ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ`, via the polynomial-ring height computation and
725	  Krull's height theorem — no transcendence-degree theory needed), through
726	* the generic glue `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`
727	  (a Noetherian local ring with `dim_κ m/m² ≤ ringKrullDim` is regular).
728	
729	The consumer `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` still carries its `sorry`
730	because its statement quantifies over *all* points `z`: at a non-closed point
731	the residue field is a transcendental extension of `k̄` and the bijectivity
732	hypothesis fails; localising this closed-point witness to a general point is
733	Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular), the one
734	remaining genuine Mathlib gap of the pipeline. Axiom-clean. -/
735	theorem isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
736	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
737	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
738	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
739	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
740	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra k Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
741	    [IsScalarTower k S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m]
742	    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))) :
743	    IsRegularLocalRing Sₘ := by
744	  haveI := hm.isPrime
745	  -- Noetherian structure: standard-smooth ⟹ finite presentation ⟹ Noetherian
746	  -- over the base field; localisation preserves Noetherianness.
747	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing k S
748	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Sₘ := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing m.primeCompl Sₘ ‹_›
749	  -- Extract the relative dimension n.
750	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ :=
751	    exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth (R := k) (S := S)
752	  haveI := hn
753	  -- Kähler package at the localisation (Stages 4, 5a, 5b).
754	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄k]) := module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
755	  haveI : Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) :=
756	    module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization m.primeCompl Sₘ
757	  have hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) = n :=
758	    rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension n m.primeCompl Sₘ
759	  -- Formal smoothness of the localisation over k.
760	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth S Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization m.primeCompl
761	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp k S Sₘ
762	  -- (ii.A): cotangent finrank = n at the k-rational closed point.
763	  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue hbij n hrank
764	  -- (ii.B): dimension lower bound n ≤ dim Sₘ.
765	  have hdim :=
766	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
767	      (k := k) n m hm Sₘ
768	  exact IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
769	    (by rw [hcot]; exact_mod_cast hdim)
770	
771	/-- **Lane COE Step B.d′ — Stacks `00TT` at closed points over an algebraically
772	closed field, unconditional form.** Over an algebraically closed base field the
773	`k`-rationality hypothesis of Step B.d is automatic: for any maximal ideal
774	`m ⊆ S` of the standard-smooth algebra `S`, the residue field `S ⧸ m` is a
775	finite (Zariski's lemma, `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing`) hence
776	algebraic extension of `k`, so `k → κ(m)` is bijective
777	(`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` composed with
778	`Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`). Hence the local ring of a
779	standard-smooth `k̄`-algebra at **every** closed point is regular. Stated for
780	the model localisation `Localization.AtPrime m`; transport to an abstract
781	localisation (e.g. a scheme stalk) via `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv` along
782	`IsLocalization.algEquiv`. Axiom-clean. -/
783	theorem isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed
784	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k]
785	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
786	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
787	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) :
788	    IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime m) := by
789	  haveI := hm.isPrime
790	  refine isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
791	    (k := k) m hm (Localization.AtPrime m) ?_
792	  -- Zariski's lemma: the residue field `S ⧸ m` is module-finite over `k`.
793	  -- (The `Field` instance must be installed FIRST so all subsequent instance
794	  -- searches on `S ⧸ m` share its semiring key.)
795	  letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m
796	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType k (S ⧸ m) :=
797	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k m) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
798	  haveI : Module.Finite k (S ⧸ m) := finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing k (S ⧸ m)
799	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k (S ⧸ m) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k (S ⧸ m)
800	  -- `k → S ⧸ m` is bijective since `k` is algebraically closed.
801	  have h1 : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) :=
802	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
803	  -- `S ⧸ m → κ(m)` is bijective since `m` is maximal.
804	  have h2 := m.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField
805	  rw [show algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (Localization.AtPrime m))
806	      = (algebraMap (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField).comp (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) from
807	    IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq k (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField]
808	  exact h2.comp h1
809	
810	/-- **Lane COE Step B.e — standard-smooth algebras over an algebraically closed
811	field are reduced (Stacks `033B`/`00TT` corollary).** For a standard-smooth
812	algebra `S` over an algebraically closed field `k`, the ring `S` is reduced.
813	
814	Route: reducedness is checked at localisations at maximal ideals
815	(`isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal`); each localisation `Sₘ` is a regular local
816	ring by the Step B.d′ closed-point theorem
817	(`isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed`),
818	regular local rings are domains
819	(`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`, the project-local
820	Stacks `00NP` from `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`), and domains are reduced.
821	The trivial-ring case is reduced vacuously. Axiom-clean. -/
822	theorem isReduced_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed
823	    (k : Type u) [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k]
824	    (S : Type u) [CommRing S] [Algebra k S]
825	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S] :
826	    _root_.IsReduced S := by
827	  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial S with
828	  | inl _ => exact ⟨fun x _ => Subsingleton.elim x 0⟩
829	  | inr _ =>
830	    refine isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal S (fun m hm => ?_)
831	    haveI := hm.isPrime
832	    haveI : IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime m) :=
833	      isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed
834	        (k := k) m hm
835	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.AtPrime m) :=
836	      RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal _
837	    infer_instance
838	
839	/-- **Stacks `056S`/`033B` over an algebraically closed field: a scheme smooth
840	over `Spec k̄` is reduced.** This discharges the former Mathlib-gap helper
841	`isReduced_of_smooth_over_field` in `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (the
842	`IsReduced A.left` input to `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`).
843	
844	Route: reducedness is stalk-local (`isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`). At each
845	point `z`, Stage 2 (`exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth`) produces an affine
846	chart `V ∋ z` over an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k̄` with
847	`Γ(X.left, V)` standard-smooth over `Γ(Spec k̄, U)`; the base identification
848	`Γ(Spec k̄, U) ≃+* k̄` (`gammaSpecField_ringEquiv`, `U` is nonempty since it
849	contains the image of `z`) transports standard-smoothness to a `k̄`-algebra
850	structure (`RingHom.isStandardSmooth_respectsIso`), Step B.e above gives
851	`IsReduced Γ(X.left, V)`, and the stalk — the localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)`
852	at the prime of `z` (`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`) — is reduced by
853	`isReduced_localizationPreserves`.
854	
855	Unlike the still-open regularity keystone `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
856	(blocked on Stacks `00OF` at non-closed points), reducedness only needs the
857	maximal-ideal localisations of the chart ring, so the closed-point theorem
858	B.d′ suffices at EVERY point `z`. Axiom-clean. -/
859	theorem isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed
860	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
861	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
862	    [Smooth X.hom] :
863	    IsReduced X.left := by
864	  haveI hstalk : ∀ z : X.left.toPresheafedSpace,
865	      _root_.IsReduced (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
866	    intro z
867	    -- Stage 2: standard-smooth chart at z, RingHom form.
868	    obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hSS⟩ := exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
869	    -- Base identification: U contains the image of z, so Γ(Spec k̄, U) ≃+* k̄.
870	    let ε : kbar ≃+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) :=
871	      (gammaSpecField_ringEquiv kbar U ⟨⟨_, e hzV⟩⟩).symm
872	    -- Transport standard-smoothness along the base iso.
873	    have hSS' : ((X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.comp ε.toRingHom).IsStandardSmooth :=
874	      RingHom.isStandardSmooth_respectsIso.2 _ ε hSS
875	    letI : Algebra kbar Γ(X.left, V) :=
876	      ((X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.comp ε.toRingHom).toAlgebra
877	    haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth kbar Γ(X.left, V) := hSS'.toAlgebra
878	    -- Sections over V are reduced (Step B.e).

309:lemma exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField
310-    [PerfectField k] [Algebra.EssFiniteType k K] :

theorem transcendental_adjoin_iff [Nontrivial A] {s : Set ι} {i : ι} :
    Transcendental (adjoin R (x '' s)) (x i) ↔ i ∉ s := by
  rw [← Set.disjoint_singleton_right]
  convert! ← hx.adjoin_iff_disjoint (t := { i })
  rw [algebraicIndependent_singleton_iff ⟨i, rfl⟩]

end AlgebraicIndependent

open Cardinal in
theorem lift_trdeg_add_le [Nontrivial R] [FaithfulSMul R S] [FaithfulSMul S A] :
    lift.{v} (trdeg R S) + lift.{u} (trdeg S A) ≤ lift.{u} (trdeg R A) := by
  simp_rw [trdeg, lift_iSup bddAbove_of_small]
  simp_rw [Cardinal.ciSup_add_ciSup _ bddAbove_of_small _ bddAbove_of_small,
    add_comm (lift.{v, u} _), ← mk_sum]
  refine ciSup_le fun ⟨s, hs⟩ ↦ ciSup_le fun ⟨t, ht⟩ ↦ ?_
  have := hs.sumElim_comp ht
  refine le_ciSup_of_le bddAbove_of_small ⟨_, this.to_subtype_range⟩ ?_
  rw [← lift_umax, mk_range_eq_of_injective this.injective, lift_id']

theorem trdeg_add_le [Nontrivial R] {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [Algebra S A]
    [FaithfulSMul R S] [FaithfulSMul S A] [IsScalarTower R S A] :
    trdeg R S + trdeg S A ≤ trdeg R A := by
  rw [← (trdeg R S).lift_id, ← (trdeg S A).lift_id, ← (trdeg R A).lift_id]
  exact lift_trdeg_add_le

/-- If for each `i : ι`, `f_i : R[X]` is transcendental over `R`, then `{f_i(X_i) | i : ι}`
in `MvPolynomial ι R` is algebraically independent over `R`. -/
theorem MvPolynomial.algebraicIndependent_polynomial_aeval_X
    (f : ι → Polynomial R) (hf : ∀ i, Transcendental R (f i)) :
    AlgebraicIndependent R fun i ↦ Polynomial.aeval (X i : MvPolynomial ι R) (f i) := by

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.WeilDivisor
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CoheightBridge
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AuslanderBuchsbaum
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension

/-!
# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)

This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:

* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
  outside this iter's scope.

These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.

## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)

Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.

The 6 pinned declarations are:

1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension

/-!
# Regularity of standard-smooth algebras at all primes (Stacks 00TT, Serre-free route)

This file closes the residual "non-closed point" gap of the smooth ⟹ regular
pipeline of `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (Stacks tag `00TT`) **without**
Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular via Serre's
homological characterisation, which is absent from Mathlib at v4.31). Instead
of localising a closed-point regularity witness, we prove regularity at an
arbitrary prime `q` of a standard-smooth algebra `S` over a *perfect* field
`k` directly, via the conormal sequence and a transcendence-degree/height
inequality:

* `MvPolynomial.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCard_le_height_add` (**Lemma A**): for
  any prime `P` of `k[x_i : i ∈ ι]` (`ι` finite) there is `d : ℕ` with
  `d ≤ trdeg k (k[xᵢ] ⧸ P)` and `#ι ≤ ht P + d`. Induction on the number of
  variables: the inductive step contracts `P ⊆ R[X]` to `p = P ∩ R`, splits
  the fiber prime into the extension case (`P = pR[X]`, where the residue
  algebra gains one transcendental element) and the finite case (where the
  fiber contributes `+1` to the height), and uses the dimension formula for
  flat (going-down) extensions `ht P = ht p + ht (P/pR[X])` (Stacks `00ON`,
  `Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown`). No Noether
  normalisation is needed.
* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCast_le_height_add`
  (**Lemma B**): the transfer of Lemma A to a standard-smooth algebra `S` of
  relative dimension `n`: for any prime `q ⊆ S` there is `d : ℕ` with
  `d ≤ trdeg k (S ⧸ q)` and `n ≤ ht q + d`. Same pullback pattern as the
  closed-point `natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal` (Krull's height theorem
  bounds the defect by the number of relations of a submersive presentation).
* `Algebra.rank_kaehlerDifferential_eq_trdeg` (**Lemma C**): for an
  essentially-finite-type field extension `K` of a perfect field `k`,
  `rank_K Ω[K⁄k] = trdeg k K`. Choose a separating transcendence basis `s`
  (`exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField`); over the
  rational-function subfield `F = k(s)` the extension is formally étale, so
  `Ω[K⁄k] ≃ K ⊗_F Ω[F⁄k]`, and `Ω[F⁄k]` is the localised module of
  `Ω[k[s]⁄k]`, free with basis `{d xᵢ}`.
* `finrank_cotangentSpace_add_finrank_kaehler_residueField` (**Lemma D**): the
  conormal (Stacks `02JK`) dimension count at an arbitrary prime: for a local
  algebra `Sₘ` formally smooth over `R` with formally smooth residue field
  `κ` and `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` free of rank `n`,
  `dim_κ (m/m²) + dim_κ Ω[κ⁄R] = n`. This generalises the iter-199
  closed-point computation (`Subsingleton Ω[κ⁄R]` forced `dim_κ m/m² = n`):
  the retraction from `FormallySmooth R κ` still makes the conormal map
  `m/m² → κ ⊗ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` (split) injective, and the cokernel is `Ω[κ⁄R]` by
  exactness (`exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange`) plus surjectivity of
  `mapBaseChange`; rank–nullity gives the identity.
* `isRegularLocalRing_of_isLocalization_atPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_perfectField`
  (**Theorem**): combining B, C, D: at any prime `q` of a standard-smooth
  `k`-algebra (`k` perfect, e.g. algebraically closed), the localisation `S_q`
  is a regular local ring. Indeed `dim_κ (m/m²) = n - dim_κ Ω[κ⁄k]`
  `= n - trdeg k κ ≤ ht q = dim S_q`, and a Noetherian local ring with
  `dim_κ m/m² ≤ dim` is regular
  (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`).

The consumer is `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` in
`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (the Stage-6 keystone of the codim-1
extension pipeline), which instantiates the theorem at the stalk of a smooth
variety over an algebraically closed field.

Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`
(nodes `lem:mvpoly_trdeg_height`, `lem:standard_smooth_trdeg_height`,
`lem:kaehler_rank_eq_trdeg`, `lem:conormal_finrank_identity`,
`thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime`).
-/

universe u v

set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3

open Ideal

section TrdegHelpers

variable {k : Type u} [Field k]

/-- Adjoining a polynomial variable to a domain raises the transcendence degree
by (at least) one: `trdeg k A + 1 ≤ trdeg k A[X]`. Superadditivity of `trdeg`
in the tower `k → A → A[X]` plus `trdeg A A[X] = 1`. -/
private lemma trdeg_add_one_le_trdeg_polynomial
    (A : Type v) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [Algebra k A] :
    Algebra.trdeg k A + 1 ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial A) := by
  haveI : FaithfulSMul k A :=
    (faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective k A).mpr (algebraMap k A).injective
  haveI : FaithfulSMul A (Polynomial A) :=
    (faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective A (Polynomial A)).mpr
      (Polynomial.C_injective)
  have h := Algebra.trdeg_add_le (R := k) (S := A) (A := Polynomial A)
  rwa [Polynomial.trdeg_of_isDomain] at h

/-- If a prime `Q ⊆ A[X]` contracts to `⊥` in `A`, the residue algebra
`A[X] ⧸ Q` contains (a copy of) `A`; hence `trdeg k A ≤ trdeg k (A[X] ⧸ Q)`. -/
private lemma trdeg_le_trdeg_polynomial_quotient
    (A : Type v) [CommRing A] [Algebra k A]
    (Q : Ideal (Polynomial A)) (hQA : Q.comap (algebraMap A (Polynomial A)) = ⊥) :
    Algebra.trdeg k A ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial A ⧸ Q) := by
  refine Algebra.trdeg_le_of_injective
    ((Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k Q).comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom k A (Polynomial A))) ?_
  rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
  intro a ha
  simp only [AlgHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_eq_mk,
    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] at ha
  have : a ∈ Q.comap (algebraMap A (Polynomial A)) := ha
  rw [hQA] at this
  simpa using this

/-- Quotienting by `⊥` does not change the transcendence degree (one
inequality suffices downstream). -/
private lemma trdeg_le_trdeg_quotient_bot
    (B : Type v) [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] :
    Algebra.trdeg k B ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (B ⧸ (⊥ : Ideal B)) := by
  refine Algebra.trdeg_le_of_injective (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k ⊥) ?_
  rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
  intro b hb
  simpa [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] using hb

/-- Transcendence degree is invariant under `k`-algebra isomorphisms (one
inequality suffices downstream). -/
private lemma trdeg_le_of_algEquiv
    {B : Type v} {C : Type v} [CommRing B] [CommRing C] [Algebra k B] [Algebra k C]
    (g : B ≃ₐ[k] C) : Algebra.trdeg k B ≤ Algebra.trdeg k C :=
  Algebra.trdeg_le_of_injective g.toAlgHom g.injective

end TrdegHelpers

section PolynomialPrime

/-! ### Lemma A: the trdeg–height inequality at primes of polynomial rings -/

/-- **Lemma A (trdeg–height inequality in finite polynomial rings).** For a
field `k`, a finite index type `ι` and a prime ideal `P ⊆ k[xᵢ : i ∈ ι]`,
there is a natural number `d` with `d ≤ trdeg k (k[xᵢ] ⧸ P)` and
`#ι ≤ ht P + d`. (Classically `d` can be taken to be the transcendence degree
of the residue field and both inequalities are equalities; the one-sided
witness form is all the regularity pipeline needs and keeps the induction
free of fraction fields.)

Induction on `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`. In the inductive step
`P ⊆ R[X]` (`R = k[xᵢ : i ∈ α]`) contracts to `p = P ∩ R`, and Stacks `00ON`
(`Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown`, applicable since
`R[X]` is free hence flat hence going-down over `R`) gives
`ht P = ht p + ht P̄` for the fiber prime `P̄ ⊆ R[X]/pR[X] ≅ (R⧸p)[X]`. If
`P̄ = ⊥` then `R[X]⧸P ≅ (R⧸p)[X]` gains one transcendental element; otherwise
`ht P̄ ≥ 1` and `R⧸p` still embeds in the residue algebra. -/
theorem MvPolynomial.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCard_le_height_add
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ι : Type v} [Finite ι]
    (P : Ideal (MvPolynomial ι k)) (hP : P.IsPrime) :
    ∃ d : ℕ, (d : Cardinal) ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (MvPolynomial ι k ⧸ P) ∧
      (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) ≤ P.height + d := by
  induction ι using Finite.induction_empty_option with
  | of_equiv e IH =>
    rename_i α β _
    haveI := hP
    set ψ : MvPolynomial α k ≃ₐ[k] MvPolynomial β k := MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k e with hψ
    set P₀ : Ideal (MvPolynomial α k) := P.comap (ψ : MvPolynomial α k →+* MvPolynomial β k)
      with hP₀
    haveI hP₀p : P₀.IsPrime := Ideal.comap_isPrime _ P
    obtain ⟨d, hd, hht⟩ := IH P₀ hP₀p
    have hmap : P = P₀.map (ψ : MvPolynomial α k →+* MvPolynomial β k) := by
      rw [hP₀, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ ψ.surjective]
    refine ⟨d, ?_, ?_⟩
    · exact le_trans hd (trdeg_le_of_algEquiv (Ideal.quotientEquivAlg P₀ P ψ hmap))
    · have hh : P₀.height = P.height := by
        rw [hP₀]
        exact RingEquiv.height_comap ψ.toRingEquiv P
      rwa [← Nat.card_congr e, ← hh]
  | h_empty =>
    exact ⟨0, by simp, by simp⟩
  | h_option IH =>
    rename_i α _
    haveI := hP
    -- Transport along `MvPolynomial (Option α) k ≃ₐ[k] (MvPolynomial α k)[X]`.
    set R := MvPolynomial α k with hR
    set ψ : MvPolynomial (Option α) k ≃ₐ[k] Polynomial R :=
      MvPolynomial.optionEquivLeft k α with hψdef
    set P' : Ideal (Polynomial R) := P.map (ψ : MvPolynomial (Option α) k →+* Polynomial R)
      with hP'def
    have hmapP : P' = P.map (ψ : MvPolynomial (Option α) k →+* Polynomial R) := rfl
    haveI hP' : P'.IsPrime := by
      rw [hmapP]
      exact Ideal.map_isPrime_of_equiv ψ.toRingEquiv
    have hPheight : P.height = P'.height := by
      rw [hmapP]
      exact (RingEquiv.height_map ψ.toRingEquiv P).symm
    -- The quotient transport `MvPolynomial (Option α) k ⧸ P ≃ₐ[k] R[X] ⧸ P'`.
    have gP : (MvPolynomial (Option α) k ⧸ P) ≃ₐ[k] (Polynomial R ⧸ P') :=
      Ideal.quotientEquivAlg P P' ψ hmapP
    -- Contract to `R`.
    set p : Ideal R := P'.comap (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)) with hpdef
    haveI hp : p.IsPrime := Ideal.comap_isPrime _ P'
    haveI : P'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
    obtain ⟨d, hd, hht⟩ := IH p hp
    -- Stacks 00ON: `ht P' = ht p + ht P̄` (going-down for the flat `R → R[X]`).
    haveI : Module.Flat R (Polynomial R) := Module.Flat.of_free
    have h00 := Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown p P'
    -- Identify the fiber ring with `(R⧸p)[X]`.
    set A := R ⧸ p with hA
    haveI : IsDomain A := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p
    have halg : algebraMap R (Polynomial R) = Polynomial.C := rfl
    set P₁ : Ideal (Polynomial R ⧸ (p.map (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)))) :=
      P'.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (p.map (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)))) with hP₁def
    have hker_le : p.map (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)) ≤ P' := by
      rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
    haveI hP₁p : P₁.IsPrime := by
      rw [hP₁def]
      exact Ideal.map_isPrime_of_surjective Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
        (by rwa [Ideal.mk_ker])
    -- The fiber equivalence `(R⧸p)[X] ≃+* R[X] ⧸ pR[X]` and the induced prime `Q`.
    have e2 : Polynomial A ≃+* (Polynomial R ⧸ (p.map (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)))) := by
      rw [halg]
      exact p.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial
    have hcomm : ∀ r : R, e2 (Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r)) =
        Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C r) := by
      intro r
      have hsymm : e2.symm (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C r)) =
          Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r) := by

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension

/-!
# Regularity of standard-smooth algebras at all primes (Stacks 00TT, Serre-free route)

This file closes the residual "non-closed point" gap of the smooth ⟹ regular
pipeline of `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (Stacks tag `00TT`) **without**
Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular, via Serre's
homological characterisation — absent from Mathlib at v4.31). Instead of
localising a closed-point regularity witness, we prove regularity at an
arbitrary prime `q` of a standard-smooth algebra `S` over a *perfect* field
`k` directly, via the conormal sequence and a transcendence-degree/height
inequality:

* `Polynomial.step_height_trdeg_of_isPrime` (**step lemma**): for a prime
  `P ⊆ R[X]` over `p = P ∩ R` (`R` Noetherian domain-free hypotheses), either
  `ht p + 1 ≤ ht P` and `trdeg k (R⧸p) ≤ trdeg k (R[X]⧸P)`, or
  `ht p = ht P` and `trdeg k (R⧸p) + 1 ≤ trdeg k (R[X]⧸P)`. The height
  identity is Stacks `00ON`
  (`Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown`; `R[X]` is free,
  hence flat, hence going-down over `R`), and the fiber prime in
  `R[X]/pR[X] ≅ (R⧸p)[X]` is either `⊥` (the residue algebra gains a
  transcendental element) or nonzero (height `≥ 1`).
* `MvPolynomial.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCard_le_height_add` (**Lemma A**): for
  any prime `P` of `k[xᵢ : i ∈ ι]` (`ι` finite) there is `d : ℕ` with
  `d ≤ trdeg k (k[xᵢ] ⧸ P)` and `#ι ≤ ht P + d`. Induction on `ι` via the
  step lemma. No Noether normalisation is needed.
* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCast_le_height_add`
  (**Lemma B**): the transfer to a standard-smooth algebra `S` of relative
  dimension `n`: for any prime `q ⊆ S` there is `d : ℕ` with
  `d ≤ trdeg k (S ⧸ q)` and `n ≤ ht q + d`. Same pullback pattern as the
  closed-point `natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal` in
  `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` (Krull's height theorem bounds the height
  defect by the number of relations of a submersive presentation).
* `Algebra.rank_kaehlerDifferential_eq_trdeg` (**Lemma C**): for an
  essentially-finite-type field extension `K` of a perfect field `k`,
  `rank_K Ω[K⁄k] = trdeg k K`. Choose a separating transcendence basis `s`
  (`exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField`); over the
  rational-function subfield `F = k(s)` the extension is formally étale, so
  `Ω[K⁄k] ≃ K ⊗_F Ω[F⁄k]`, and `Ω[F⁄k]` is the localised module of
  `Ω[k[s]⁄k]`, free with basis `{d xᵢ}`.
* `finrank_cotangentSpace_add_finrank_kaehler_residueField` (**Lemma D**): the
  conormal (Stacks `02JK`) dimension count at an arbitrary prime: for a local
  algebra `Sₘ` formally smooth over `R` with formally smooth residue field
  `κ` and `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` free of rank `n`,
  `dim_κ (m/m²) + dim_κ Ω[κ⁄R] = n`. This generalises the iter-199
  closed-point computation in `CodimOneExtension.lean` (there
  `Subsingleton Ω[κ⁄R]` forced `dim_κ m/m² = n`): the retraction from
  `FormallySmooth R κ` still makes the conormal map `m/m² → κ ⊗ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`
  (split) injective, the cokernel is `Ω[κ⁄R]` by exactness
  (`exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange`) plus surjectivity of
  `mapBaseChange`, and rank–nullity gives the identity.
* `isRegularLocalRing_of_isLocalization_atPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_perfectField`
  (**Theorem**, Stacks `00TT` at every prime): combining B, C, D: at any
  prime `q` of a standard-smooth `k`-algebra (`k` perfect, e.g. algebraically
  closed), any localisation `S_q` is a regular local ring. Indeed
  `dim_κ (m/m²) = n - dim_κ Ω[κ⁄k] = n - trdeg k κ ≤ ht q = dim S_q`, and a
  Noetherian local ring with `dim_κ m/m² ≤ dim` is regular
  (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim` from
  `StandardSmoothDimension.lean`).

The consumer is `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` in
`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (the Stage-6 keystone of the codim-1
extension pipeline), which instantiates the theorem at the stalk of a smooth
variety over an algebraically closed field.

Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`
(nodes `lem:polynomial_step_height_trdeg`, `lem:mvpoly_trdeg_height`,
`lem:standard_smooth_trdeg_height`, `lem:kaehler_rank_eq_trdeg`,
`lem:conormal_finrank_identity`, `thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime`).
-/

universe u v

set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3

open Ideal

section TrdegHelpers

variable {k : Type u} [Field k]

/-- Adjoining a polynomial variable to a domain raises the transcendence degree
by (at least) one: `trdeg k A + 1 ≤ trdeg k A[X]`. Superadditivity of `trdeg`
in the tower `k → A → A[X]` plus `trdeg A A[X] = 1`. -/
private lemma trdeg_add_one_le_trdeg_polynomial
    (A : Type v) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [Algebra k A] :
    Algebra.trdeg k A + 1 ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial A) := by
  haveI : FaithfulSMul k A :=
    (faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective k A).mpr (algebraMap k A).injective
  haveI : FaithfulSMul A (Polynomial A) :=
    (faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective A (Polynomial A)).mpr
      Polynomial.C_injective
  have h := Algebra.trdeg_add_le (R := k) (S := A) (A := Polynomial A)
  rwa [Polynomial.trdeg_of_isDomain] at h

/-- If a prime `Q ⊆ A[X]` contracts to `⊥` in `A`, the residue algebra
`A[X] ⧸ Q` contains (a copy of) `A`; hence `trdeg k A ≤ trdeg k (A[X] ⧸ Q)`. -/
private lemma trdeg_le_trdeg_polynomial_quotient
    (A : Type v) [CommRing A] [Algebra k A]
    (Q : Ideal (Polynomial A)) (hQA : Q.comap (algebraMap A (Polynomial A)) = ⊥) :
    Algebra.trdeg k A ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial A ⧸ Q) := by
  refine Algebra.trdeg_le_of_injective
    ((Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k Q).comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom k A (Polynomial A))) ?_
  rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
  intro a ha
  simp only [AlgHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_eq_mk,
    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] at ha
  have h1 : a ∈ Q.comap (algebraMap A (Polynomial A)) := ha
  rw [hQA] at h1
  simpa using h1

/-- Transcendence degree is monotone along `k`-algebra isomorphisms (one
inequality suffices downstream). -/
private lemma trdeg_le_of_algEquiv
    {B C : Type v} [CommRing B] [CommRing C] [Algebra k B] [Algebra k C]
    (g : B ≃ₐ[k] C) : Algebra.trdeg k B ≤ Algebra.trdeg k C :=
  Algebra.trdeg_le_of_injective g.toAlgHom g.injective

end TrdegHelpers

section PolynomialStep

/-! ### The one-variable step of Lemma A -/

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
  [Algebra k R]

/-- **One-variable step for the trdeg–height inequality.** For a prime
`P ⊆ R[X]` lying over `p = P ∩ R` in a Noetherian ring `R`, either the height
jumps (`ht p + 1 ≤ ht P`) while the residue algebra still contains `R⧸p`, or
the height is preserved (`ht P = ht p`) and the residue algebra gains a
transcendental element (`trdeg + 1`). Stacks `00ON` supplies
`ht P = ht p + ht P̄` for the fiber prime `P̄` of `R[X]/pR[X] ≅ (R⧸p)[X]`;
the two cases are `P̄ ≠ ⊥` (then `ht P̄ ≥ 1`) and `P̄ = ⊥` (then
`R[X]⧸P ≅ (R⧸p)[X]`). -/
private theorem Polynomial.step_height_trdeg_of_isPrime
    (P : Ideal (Polynomial R)) (hP : P.IsPrime) :
    letI p := P.comap (algebraMap R (Polynomial R))
    (p.height + 1 ≤ P.height ∧
      Algebra.trdeg k (R ⧸ p) ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial R ⧸ P)) ∨
    (P.height = p.height ∧
      Algebra.trdeg k (R ⧸ p) + 1 ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (Polynomial R ⧸ P)) := by
  haveI := hP
  set p : Ideal R := P.comap (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)) with hpdef
  haveI hp : p.IsPrime := Ideal.comap_isPrime _ P
  haveI : P.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
  -- Stacks 00ON (going-down for the flat extension `R → R[X]`).
  haveI : Module.Flat R (Polynomial R) := Module.Flat.of_free
  have h00 := Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown p P
  -- Notation for the fiber.
  set pX : Ideal (Polynomial R) := p.map (algebraMap R (Polynomial R)) with hpXdef
  have hker_le : pX ≤ P := Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap.mpr le_rfl
  set Pbar : Ideal (Polynomial R ⧸ pX) := P.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk pX) with hPbardef
  haveI hPbarPrime : Pbar.IsPrime :=
    Ideal.map_isPrime_of_surjective Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      (by rwa [Ideal.mk_ker])
  set A := R ⧸ p with hAdef
  haveI : IsDomain A := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p
  -- The fiber ring identification `A[X] ≃+* R[X] ⧸ pR[X]`.
  have hCeq : p.map (Polynomial.C (R := R)) = pX := by
    rw [hpXdef, Polynomial.algebraMap_eq]
  have e2 : Polynomial A ≃+* (Polynomial R ⧸ pX) :=
    p.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial.trans (Ideal.quotEquivOfEq hCeq)
  have hcomm : ∀ r : R, e2 (Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r)) =
      Ideal.Quotient.mk pX (Polynomial.C r) := by
    intro r
    have hs : p.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial.symm
        (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C r)) = Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r) := by
      rw [Ideal.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial_symm_mk, Polynomial.map_C]
    have : p.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial
        (Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r)) = Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C r) := by
      rw [← hs, RingEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
    show (Ideal.quotEquivOfEq hCeq) (p.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial
        (Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r))) = _
    rw [this, Ideal.quotEquivOfEq_mk]
  -- The fiber prime `Q ⊆ A[X]` and its basic properties.
  set Q : Ideal (Polynomial A) := Pbar.comap e2 with hQdef
  haveI hQPrime : Q.IsPrime := Ideal.comap_isPrime _ Pbar
  have hQheight : Q.height = Pbar.height := RingEquiv.height_comap e2 Pbar
  have hQmap : Pbar = Q.map (e2 : Polynomial A →+* (Polynomial R ⧸ pX)) := by
    rw [hQdef, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ e2.surjective]
  -- Membership translation between `Q` and `P`.
  have hmem : ∀ r : R, Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p r) ∈ Q ↔ Polynomial.C r ∈ P := by
    intro r
    rw [hQdef, Ideal.mem_comap, hcomm, hPbardef,
      Ideal.mem_quotient_iff_mem hker_le]
  -- The contraction of `Q` to `A` is trivial.
  have hQA : Q.comap (algebraMap A (Polynomial A)) = ⊥ := by
    ext a
    obtain ⟨r, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective a
    simp only [Ideal.mem_comap, Polynomial.algebraMap_eq, Submodule.mem_bot]
    rw [hmem r, Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem, ← Ideal.mem_comap]
    rw [show (Polynomial.C : R →+* Polynomial R) = algebraMap R (Polynomial R) from
      (Polynomial.algebraMap_eq).symm, ← hpdef]
  -- The `k`-algebra identification of residue algebras `A[X] ⧸ Q ≃ₐ[k] R[X] ⧸ P`.
  have e2alg : Polynomial A ≃ₐ[k] (Polynomial R ⧸ pX) := by
    refine AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := e2) ?_
    intro c
    have h1 : algebraMap k (Polynomial A) c =
        Polynomial.C (Ideal.Quotient.mk p (algebraMap k R c)) := by
      rw [Polynomial.algebraMap_eq' (R := k)]  -- placeholder; fixed below if needed
    sorry
  sorry

end PolynomialStep
